rationale_for_baseline_tier: With a negative partner-assisted FAST, global symmetric slowing, afebrile status, normal breathing, rousability and a stereotyped prior crash pattern, this is a severe PEM episode best managed with supportive radical rest, hydration and telehealth follow-up. An in-person/ED transfer can itself worsen the crash, so routine escalation is over-triage. T2 (supportive care + telehealth review with strong safety-netting) is correct and also the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for any FAST-positive feature, fever, unrousability or breathing change.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Facial droop, one-sided weakness, slurred speech, or any new sudden focal deficit
escalation_edges.communication_template: If her face droops, one side goes weak, or her speech slurs, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High fever, becoming unrousable, or very shallow/abnormal breathing
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates possible sepsis/encephalopathy or respiratory compromise.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she develops a high fever, can't be woken, or her breathing becomes very shallow, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to keep any fluids down, or the carer can no longer cope
escalation_edges.rationale: Dehydration risk and carer strain need timely, low-exertion support (e.g. home visit).
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she can't keep fluids down or you can't manage her care, arrange a low-exertion review — ideally a home visit — rather than a stressful ED trip.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Partner providing total care (hydration, feeding, turning)
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Support the carer, arrange home-based help where possible, and prefer telehealth/home review over ED to avoid worsening PEM.
Minimise exertion in all care decisions; avoid unnecessary travel and stimulation.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a severe post-exertional crash of your ME/CFS. We've checked for stroke and infection and they look clear, so the safest thing is complete rest in a dark, quiet room, gentle fluids, and a telehealth review — a hospital trip would likely make the crash worse. Your partner should keep you comfortable and hydrated.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Telehealth GP follow-up within about a week; call 000 immediately for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000 only for the emergency features; otherwise continue radical rest.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the exertional trigger, negative partner FAST, afebrile/rousable status, and that in-person visits are being avoided to prevent worsening PEM.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassurance with no stroke/sepsis screen and no safety-netting, potentially missing a stroke.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An early stroke is missed because no FAST was done and no escalation advice was given.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Reflexively sending a screen-negative severe PEM patient to ED, where light/noise/exertion markedly worsen and prolong the crash.
